name: Sun dog
primary_sources: Historia Naturalis by Pliny the Elder

distinctive_features: located at 22° to the left and right of the Sun
appear at the same altitude as the Sun
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: angular distance of 22° from the Sun
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: parallel to the horizon, flanking the Sun

functional_attributes.primary_function: optical phenomenon caused by refraction of sunlight

domain_specific_functional.co_occurrence: often seen with circumzenithal arc and 22° halo
domain_specific_functional.planetary_variation: can occur on other planets with different ice crystals

relationships.description: Sun dogs are caused by the refraction of sunlight through ice crystals.
Sun dogs often appear within a 22° halo.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: interpreted as weather omens and symbols in various cultures
primary_sources: Historia Naturalis by Pliny the Elder
